mnemonics.title: Truth Table Rows
mnemonics.content: Remember: 2^n rows for n variables. Think: '2 to the power of n gives the rows in view!'

mnemonics.title: Implication Rule
mnemonics.content: FALSE implies anything is TRUE. Think: 'A false promise leads to anything being acceptable' - when p is FALSE, p→q is always TRUE.

mnemonics.title: Biconditional Truth
mnemonics.content: p ↔ q is TRUE when p and q 'agree' (both T or both F). Think: 'The biconditional binds when they bind together!'

mnemonics.title: De Morgan's Laws
mnemonics.content: Break the line, switch the sign: ¬(A ∧ B) = ¬A ∨ ¬B. Think: 'NOT distributes and switches!'

acronyms.usage: Used in truth tables to denote true value or always-true proposition
Used in truth tables to denote false value or always-false proposition
Both must be true for result to be true
At least one must be true for result to be true
Inverts the truth value of a proposition

visualTips.visualization: Think of p as 'antecedent' (condition) and q as 'consequent' (result). Only fails when you have condition but no result!
Write all combinations in binary counting order: 00, 01, 10, 11 for 2 variables - systematic order prevents missing cases!
Tautology = ALL T in final column (like all green traffic lights). Contradiction = ALL F in final column (like all red lights!). Contingency = Mixed T and F (traffic signal!)

analogies.analogy: Think of AND like a series circuit - current flows only if ALL switches are closed!
Think of OR like parallel circuit - current flows if AT LEAST ONE switch is closed!
Think of p→q like a promise: 'If you study (p), then you will pass (q)'. The promise is broken only if you studied but didn't pass!
Think of NOT like a mirror - it flips true to false and false to true, just like a mirror flips your image!
